prompt: I have three videos. Edit them nicely, remove boring parts, and combine properly.
Using the attached media descriptors and notes, suggest edit order and what to cut first for a short highlight story.
descriptorContext: Image 1: LinkedIn post screenshot announcing winners (Team Vyoman first, Team AllKnighters second) with award ceremony collage.
Image 2: Close-up award ceremony photo of Team AllKnighters receiving 2nd place cybersecurity award.
Video: candid workspace clip showing the same winners at desk/laptop; natural behind-the-scenes context.

prompt: What do you see in this image? Give 5 concise observations and 3 practical QuickCut edit suggestions (crop/color/text emphasis/timing). Do not suggest removing logos/watermarks or unverifiable claims.

Analyze this short video. Return exactly with compact bullets only: 1) key actions observed (max 4 bullets), 2) low-value moments to trim with rough timestamps (max 4 bullets), 3) exactly 3 practical QuickCut edits.

Timeline snapshot: clip_1 intro (0-12s), clip_2 talking head with pauses (12-55s), clip_3 b-roll (55-78s). User says: "remove awkward pauses and make flow smoother." Return JSON only for execution readiness.
